codeReview.summary: Solid foundation setup. Next.js 16 with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4 correctly configured. All required interfaces, comprehensive placeholder data, Google Fonts (Playfair Display, Lora, Inter), zero border-radius enforcement, Unsplash image domains, and cn() utility all present and working. Build, typecheck, and lint all pass. The feature description's requirements are fully met with only minor non-blocking observations.

issues.description: Default create-next-app boilerplate SVGs (file.svg, globe.svg, next.svg, vercel.svg, window.svg) were left in the public directory. These are unused and should be cleaned up eventually to avoid confusion.

Color token naming uses generic names (--color-primary, --color-primary-dark, --color-accent) rather than descriptive names (--color-terracotta, --color-burnt-sienna, --color-olive). AGENTS.md references class names like 'bg-terracotta' and 'text-burnt-sienna' but the actual Tailwind classes will be 'bg-primary' and 'text-primary-dark'. This naming mismatch could cause confusion for downstream workers, though AGENTS.md also shows the correct 'var(--color-*)' syntax.

The `* { border-radius: 0 !important; }` rule is very aggressive. While effective, it may conflict with third-party libraries (Swiper pagination bullets already need their own !important override at line 121). A more targeted approach could avoid cascading specificity battles, but the current approach works.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md mentions Tailwind class names 'bg-terracotta, text-burnt-sienna, etc.' but the actual theme tokens are named 'primary', 'primary-dark', 'accent'. Downstream workers may be confused about which class names to use. AGENTS.md should clarify the actual Tailwind class names: bg-primary (#C65D3E terracotta), text-primary-dark (#A0522D burnt sienna), bg-accent (#6B7F3E olive).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line 'Reference colors as var(--color-*) or Tailwind classes like bg-terracotta, text-burnt-sienna, etc.' vs globals.css @theme block which defines --color-primary, --color-primary-dark, --color-accent

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted in skillFeedback that the frontend-worker skill references 'tailwind.config.ts' in preconditions for other features, but Tailwind v4 uses @theme in CSS instead. The skill should be updated to reflect this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The frontend-worker skill references tailwind.config.ts in preconditions for other features, but Tailwind v4 uses @theme in CSS instead.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description specified creating src/styles/ directory as part of the project structure, but no files were placed there — globals.css lives in src/app/globals.css (standard Next.js App Router pattern). The src/styles/ directory is empty/unused. This is fine (App Router convention), but it means future features should not expect to find styles in src/styles/.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description: 'Create the project structure: src/app/, src/components/, src/lib/, src/styles/.' But git show 5617e48 shows globals.css at src/app/globals.css with no files under src/styles/.

summary: The foundation-project-setup feature is well implemented. All core requirements are met: Next.js 16 with App Router and TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4 with Soul Good design tokens (terracotta #C65D3E, burnt sienna #A0522D, olive #6B7F3E, cream backgrounds, border gray), Google Fonts (Playfair Display, Lora, Inter) loading correctly via next/font/google, comprehensive TypeScript interfaces in types.ts, rich placeholder data in constants.ts covering all content areas, cn() utility helper, zero border-radius enforcement, and Unsplash image domain configuration. Build, typecheck, and lint all pass cleanly. Three non-blocking issues noted: leftover create-next-app boilerplate SVGs, generic color token naming vs. AGENTS.md references, and aggressive global !important on border-radius. Overall: PASS.
